finding_results.notes: Recast the flagged rapid-perception beats as grammatical close-third sentences and clauses, replacing repeated negative-contrast fragments and noun-list clipping while preserving scene speed and POV pressure.
finding_results.summary: The recurrent fragment tic was removed from the packet-flagged action and aftermath passages.

finding_results.notes: Added Jet-internal beats that tie the debrief argument to the ore-hauler near miss, the 8,000-woolong fine, and Jet's recognition of the crew's predictable fight pattern before Spike reframes the chase.
finding_results.summary: The hangar debrief now stays anchored in Jet's embarrassment, financial panic, and exasperated pattern-recognition before Spike's analysis takes over.

finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_05.md:472, chapters/chapter_05.md:554, chapters/chapter_05.md:580, chapters/chapter_05.md:590, chapters/chapter_05.md:604, chapters/chapter_05.md:670, chapters/chapter_05.md:676
finding_results.notes: Revised four areas in the hangar debrief to address Spike functioning as a theme-delivery system. (1) Replaced aphoristic 'We were' / 'It's the whole answer' at line 472 with concrete observation about the pilot's speed during formation split. (2) Replaced thesis-statement 'She wasn't running blind' at line 554 with concrete physical detail about cut chaff and hand-wired beacon; removed narrator-admiration 'The line sat there'; gave Jet the drone-wiring observation so interpretive work is distributed. (3) Broke up Spike's clean three-part diagnostic ('You got impatient / Jet tried to herd / I took the narrow cut') at lines 580-592: Jet now identifies the first two patterns (Faye would break lane, Jet would hold middle) and Spike adds only his own piece with concrete detail (chaff loaded specifically for the tunnel); removed narrator-admiration 'He said it without drama, like he was reading damage off a panel.' (4) Reworked 'Not a stranger' at line 604 so it arrives through conversation rather than a room-silencing pronouncement; removed 'That pulled the room still again.' (5) Restructured the climactic 'She's running us' at line 676: Spike now gives the observation ('She's not trying to lose us') and Jet names the conclusion ('She's running us'); removed narrator-admiration 'No one said anything after that because there was not much to add that did not sound smaller than the sentence already hanging there.' Throughout, Spike's insights now emerge through concrete observations (chaff cut to size, tunnel width, pilot's unchanged speed) rather than polished thesis statements, and the narration no longer frames his lines as definitive room-silencing conclusions.

pass_summaries.summary: Revised chapter_05 in place to fix the packet's prose and craft findings: clipped narration was normalized into grammatical close-third prose, and the hangar aftermath now carries Jet's interior consequence beats before the scene turns outward.

Single finding addressed: loosened Spike's hangar debrief dialogue from aphoristic thesis-delivery into concrete observation and distributed interpretive work to Jet and Faye. Five narrator-admiration framings removed ('The line sat there,' 'He said it without drama,' 'That pulled the room still again,' 'No one said anything...,' and the implicit framing of 'We were' as a complete answer). Spike now arrives at his reads through physical detail (cut chaff, unchanged speed, tunnel dimensions) rather than polished summary sentences. Jet makes two of the three pattern observations and names the climactic insight. All changes are local to the debrief scene (lines 472-678); chase sequences, Ed's POV section, and chapter opening/closing are untouched. Heading contract preserved.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=3, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
